title: CLAUDE.md
text: Full package check: Rscript -e "devtools::check()" Re-load package: devtools::load_all() Update documentation: devtools::document() Run tests: devtools::test() Run single test: testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-filename.R") Code coverage: covr::package_coverage() terminal, wrap commands like : Rscript -e "devtools::load_all(); <COMMAND>"

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Function naming: Use snake_case tf_ prefix exported functions Method implementation: Use ClassName.method pattern S3 methods Documentation: Use roxygen2 markdown formatting (@param, @return) Imports: Declare dependencies DESCRIPTION file; avoid tidyverse; avoid adding dependencies additional packages. Error handling: Use cli::cli_abort() cli::cli_warning() errors warnings, use checkmate-assertions custom assertions assertions.R Object creation: Follow vctrs framework S3 classes Testing: Write unit tests testthat v3 (expect_equal, expect_error) PRs: Make pull requests ‘main’ branch Always reload package devtools::load_all() testing changes.

what: S3 Classes and Methods for Tidy Functional Data
title: S3 Classes and Methods for Tidy Functional Data
text: tf package provides necessary infrastructure tidyfun minimal dependencies – specifically: tidyverse-dependencies. goal tidyfun, turn, provide accessible well-documented software makes functional data analysis R easy – specifically data wrangling exploratory analysis. tf includes definitions new S3 data types vectors functional data associated methods. tf-vectors, subclasses tfd tfb, use vctrs-framework, can operated using standard functions (+, mean(), c(), etc.) well several new functions tf implement operations specific functional data (tf_smooth, tf_derive, tf_integrate, tf_register, …). Crucially, vectors class tf can included data frames containing variables, simple reliable data manipulation. approach connected conceptual framework functional data analysis assumes complete functions unit observation. tidyfun tf, can keep full curves alongside numeric, factor, observations subject one data frame.

title: Eigenfunctions via weighted, regularized SVD — fpc_wsvd
text: list entries mu estimated mean function (numeric vector) efunctions estimated FPCs (numeric matrix, columns represent FPCs) scores estimated FPC scores (one row per observed curve) npc many FPCs returned given pve (integer) scoring_function function returns FPC scores new data given eigenfunctions, see tf:::.fpc_wsvd_scores example.

title: Eigenfunctions via weighted, regularized SVD — fpc_wsvd
text: Performs weighted SVD trapezoidal quadrature weights s.t. returned vectors represent (evaluations ) orthonormal eigenfunctions \(\phi_j(t)\), eigenvectors \(\phi_j = (\phi_j(t_1), \dots, \phi_j(t_n))\), specifically: \(\int_T \phi_j(t)^2 dt \approx \sum_i \Delta_i \phi_j(t_i)^2 = 1\) given quadrature weights \(\Delta_i\), \(\phi_j'\phi_j = \sum_i \phi_j(t_i)^2 = 1\); \(\int_T \phi_j(t) \phi_k(t) dt = 0\) \(\phi_j'\phi_k = \sum_i \phi_j(t_i)\phi_k(t_i) = 0\), c.f. mogsa::wsvd(). incomplete data, uses adaptation softImpute::softImpute(), see references. Note work well data common grid percent data points missing, breaks completely truly irregular data /common timepoints, even observed densely. data, either re-evaluate common grid first use advanced FPCA approaches like refund::fpca_sc(), see last example tfb_fpc()

title: Summarize each tf in a vector (function-wise) — functionwise
text: tf_fwise(): User-specified function-wise summary statistics tf_fmax(): maximal value function tf_fmin(): minimal value function tf_fmedian(): median value function tf_frange(): range values function tf_fmean(): mean function: \(\tfrac{1}{|T|}\int_T x_i(t) dt\) tf_fvar(): variance function: \(\tfrac{1}{|T|}\int_T (x_i(t) - \bar x(t))^2 dt\) tf_fsd(): standard deviation function: \(\sqrt{\tfrac{1}{|T|}\int_T (x_i(t) - \bar x(t))^2 dt}\) tf_crosscov(): cross-covariances two functional vectors: \(\tfrac{1}{|T|}\int_T (x_i(t) - \bar x(t)) (y_i(t)-\bar y(t)) dt\) tf_crosscor(): cross-correlation two functional vectors: tf_crosscov(x, y) / (tf_fsd(x) * tf_fsd(y))

title: Summarize each tf in a vector (function-wise) — functionwise
code: x <- tf_rgp(3) layout(t(1:3)) plot(x, col = 1:3) #  each function's values to [0,1]: x_clamp <- (x - tf_fmin(x)) / (tf_fmax(x) - tf_fmin(x)) plot(x_clamp, col = 1:3) # standardize each function to have mean / integral 0 and sd 1: x_std <- (x - tf_fmean(x)) / tf_fsd(x) tf_fvar(x_std) == c(1, 1, 1) #> [1]  TRUE FALSE  TRUE plot(x_std, col = 1:3)  # Custom functions: # 80%tiles of each function's values: tf_fwise(x, \(.x) quantile(.x$value, 0.8)) |> unlist() #>       1.80%       2.80%       3.80%  #>  0.54956138 -0.06731273  1.15582713  # minimal value of each function for t > 0.5 tf_fwise(x, \(.x) min(.x$value[.x$arg > 0.5])) |> unlist() #>           1           2           3  #> -0.04494065 -2.82525685 -0.17442921   tf_crosscor(x, -x) #> [1] -1 -1 -1 tf_crosscov(x, x) == tf_fvar(x) #> [1] TRUE TRUE TRUE

title: Functional Data Depth — tf_depth
text: vector depth values

title: Functional Data Depth — tf_depth
text: "MBD": Modified Band-2 Depth (default). Scale 0 (extreme) 1 (central). "MHI": Modified Hypograph Index. Ranks functions lowest (0) highest (1) instead extreme central! functions never cross: \(MBD = -2(MHI - 0.5)^2 + .5\). "FM": Fraiman-Muniz depth. Integrates pointwise univariate halfspace depths domain. Scale 0 (extreme) 1 (central). "FSD": Functional Spatial Depth. Based spatial signs; robust outliers. Scale 0 (extreme) 1 (central). "RPD": Regularized Projection Depth. Projects curves onto random directions computes outlyingness. Especially useful detecting shape outliers. Scale 0 (extreme) 1 (central). Note: results depend RNG state; set seed (e.g. set.seed(...)) calling reproducibility. Accepts additional arguments via ...: u (quantile level regularization, default 0.01), n_projections (M, number projection directions, default 5000), n_projections_beta (L, directions estimating regularization parameter, default 500).

Domain grid returned object identical input. Unless tfd rather fine regular grid, representing data suitable basis representation tfb() computing derivatives (integrals) usually preferable. title: Estimate warping functions for registration — tf_estimate_warps
text: x tf vector functions register. ... additional method-specific arguments passed backend routines (example crit method = "cc"). template optional tf vector length 1 use template. NULL, default template computed (method-dependent). used method = "landmark". method registration method use: "srvf": Square Root Velocity Framework (elastic registration). details, see fdasrvf::time_warping(). Default template Karcher mean. "cc": continuous-criterion registration via tf-native dense-grid optimizer monotone spline warps. Default template arithmetic mean. "affine": affine (linear) registration warps form \(h(t) = \cdot t + b\). Simpler elastic registration, appropriate phase variability consists shifts /uniform speed-/slow-. Default template arithmetic mean. "landmark": piecewise-linear warps align user-specified landmark features. Requires landmarks argument. max_iter integer: maximum number Procrustes-style template refinement iterations template = NULL. iteration cycle : (1) estimate template mean (aligned) curves, (2) register curves current template, (3) update template mean newly aligned curves, (4) repeat convergence max_iter reached. Ignored template provided (refinement needed) method = "landmark" (template used). method = "srvf" template = NULL, outer Procrustes loop skipped regardless max_iter fdasrvf::time_warping() already computes Karcher mean internally. Default 3L. tol numeric: convergence tolerance template refinement. method = "cc", iteration stops relative improvement registration criterion becomes negligible; iterative methods, iteration stops relative change template (L2 norm) falls tol. Default 1e-2.

title: Estimate warping functions for registration — tf_estimate_warps
text: tfd vector (forward) warping functions \(h_i(s) = t\) length x. Apply tf_align() obtain registered functions, use tf_invert() obtain inverse warps \(h_i^{-1}(t) = s\). returned warps carry attr(, "template") template used (NULL landmark registration, template).

title: Estimate warping functions for registration — tf_estimate_warps
text: method = "cc", tf uses tf-native dense-grid optimizer monotone spline warps. warp represented normalized cumulative integral exp(eta(t)), eta(t) spline nbasis coefficients. Registration carried curve--curve minimizing either integrated squared-error criterion (crit = 1) first-eigenfunction variance criterion (crit = 2) plus optional spline roughness penalty (lambda). outer max_iter loop, template = NULL, still performs Procrustes-style template refinement methods.

title: Estimate warping functions for registration — tf_estimate_warps
text: method = "srvf": lambda non-negative number: penalty controlling flexibility warpings (default 0 unrestricted warps). penalty_method cost function used penalize warping functions. Defaults "roughness" (norm second derivative), "geodesic" uses geodesic distance identity "norm" uses Euclidean distance identity. method = "cc": nbasis integer: number B-spline basis functions monotone warp basis (default 6L, minimum 2). lambda non-negative number: roughness penalty warp basis (default 0 unpenalized warping). crit registration criterion. Defaults 2 first-eigenfunction variance criterion; alternative 1 integrated squared error. conv non-negative convergence tolerance inner optimizer. Default 1e-4. iterlim maximum number inner optimization iterations per curve. Default 20L. method = "affine": type character: "shift" (translation ), "scale" (scaling ), "shift_scale" (). Default "shift". shift_range numeric(2): bounds shift parameter. Default c(-range/2, range/2) range domain width. Larger bounds allow greater shifts may result NA values. scale_range numeric(2): bounds scale parameter. Default c(0.5, 2). Must lower > 0. method = "landmark": landmarks (required) numeric matrix landmark positions one row per function one column per landmark. Use tf_landmarks_extrema() find peaks/valleys automatically. template_landmarks numeric vector target landmark positions. Default column-wise mean landmarks.

title: Estimate warping functions for registration — tf_estimate_warps
text: Maximilian Muecke, Fabian Scheipl, Claude Opus 4.6

title: Register / align a tf vector against a template function — tf_register
text: tf_register() high-level entry point functional data registration. estimates warping functions, applies align data, returns tf_registration result object containing aligned curves, inverse warping functions (observed aligned time), template. Use tf_aligned(), tf_inv_warps(), tf_template() extract components.

title: Register / align a tf vector against a template function — tf_register
code: tf_register(   x,   ...,   template = NULL,   method = c("srvf", "cc", "affine", "landmark"),   max_iter = 3L,   tol = 0.01,   store_x = TRUE )

title: Register / align a tf vector against a template function — tf_register
text: x tf vector functions register. ... additional method-specific arguments passed backend routines (example crit method = "cc"). See tf_estimate_warps() method-specific argument documentation. template optional tf vector length 1 use template. NULL, default template computed (method-dependent). used method = "landmark". method registration method use: "srvf": Square Root Velocity Framework (elastic registration). "cc": continuous-criterion registration via tf-native dense-grid optimizer monotone spline warps. "affine": affine (linear) registration. "landmark": piecewise-linear warps aligning user-specified landmarks. max_iter integer: maximum Procrustes-style template refinement iterations. Default 3L. tol numeric: convergence tolerance template refinement. Default 1e-2. store_x logical: store original data result object? Default TRUE. Set FALSE save memory.

title: Register / align a tf vector against a template function — tf_register
text: tf_registration object. Access components tf_aligned(), tf_inv_warps(), tf_template().

title: Register / align a tf vector against a template function — tf_register
text: lower-level interface returns warping functions (without performing alignment), see tf_estimate_warps().

title: Register / align a tf vector against a template function — tf_register
text: method = "srvf": lambda non-negative number: penalty controlling flexibility warpings (default 0 unrestricted warps). penalty_method cost function used penalize warping functions. Defaults "roughness" (norm second derivative), "geodesic" uses geodesic distance identity "norm" uses Euclidean distance identity. method = "cc": nbasis integer: number B-spline basis functions monotone warp basis (default 6L, minimum 2). lambda non-negative number: roughness penalty warp basis (default 0 unpenalized warping). crit registration criterion. Defaults 2 first-eigenfunction variance criterion; alternative 1 integrated squared error. conv non-negative convergence tolerance inner optimizer. Default 1e-4. iterlim maximum number inner optimization iterations per curve. Default 20L. method = "affine": type character: "shift" (translation ), "scale" (scaling ), "shift_scale" (). Default "shift". shift_range numeric(2): bounds shift parameter. Default c(-range/2, range/2) range domain width. Larger bounds allow greater shifts may result NA values. scale_range numeric(2): bounds scale parameter. Default c(0.5, 2). Must lower > 0. method = "landmark": landmarks (required) numeric matrix landmark positions one row per function one column per landmark. Use tf_landmarks_extrema() find peaks/valleys automatically. template_landmarks numeric vector target landmark positions. Default column-wise mean landmarks.

title: Register / align a tf vector against a template function — tf_register
text: Ramsay JO, Hooker G, Graves S (2009). Functional Data Analysis R MATLAB. Springer, New York. doi:10.1007/978-0-387-98185-7 . Srivastava , Wu W, Kurtek S, Klassen E, Marron JS (2011). "Registration Functional Data Using Fisher-Rao Metric." arXiv:1103.3817. Tucker JD, Wu W, Srivastava (2013). "Generative models functional data using phase amplitude separation." Computational Statistics & Data Analysis, 61, 50–66. doi:10.1016/j.csda.2012.12.001 .

title: Register / align a tf vector against a template function — tf_register
text: Maximilian Muecke, Fabian Scheipl, Claude Opus 4.6

title: Register / align a tf vector against a template function — tf_register
code: # Elastic registration (SRVF method) height_female <- subset(growth, gender == "female", select = height, drop = TRUE) growth_female <- tf_derive(height_female) |> tfd(arg = seq(1.125, 17.8), l = 101) reg <- tf_register(growth_female) layout(t(1:3)) plot(growth_female, xlab = "Chronological Age", ylab = "Growth Rate (cm/year)") plot(tf_inv_warps(reg), xlab = "Chronological Age", ylab = "Biological Age") plot(tf_aligned(reg), xlab = "Biological Age", ylab = "Growth Rate (cm/year)")  # Affine registration (shift only) t <- seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = 101) x <- tfd(t(sapply(c(-0.3, 0, 0.3), function(s) sin(t + s))), arg = t) reg <- tf_register(x, method = "affine", type = "shift") #> Warning: ℹ 10 evaluations were `NA` #> ✖ Returning irregular <tfd>. #> Warning: ℹ 10 evaluations were `NA` #> ✖ Returning irregular <tfd>. #> Warning: ℹ 10 evaluations were `NA` #> ✖ Returning irregular <tfd>. plot(tf_aligned(reg), col = 1:3)  # Landmark registration peaks <- tf_landmarks_extrema(x, "max") reg <- tf_register(x, method = "landmark", landmarks = peaks) plot(tf_aligned(reg), col = 1:3)

Accessors return respective component.

title: Registration Result Object — tf_registration
text: summary() computes per-curve diagnostics assessing registration quality prints averages /deciles. printed output contains four sections: Amplitude variance reduction (store_x = TRUE): proportion pointwise variance removed registration, computed \(1 - \bar{V}_{\mathrm{registered}} / \bar{V}_{\mathrm{original}}\) \(\bar{V}\) mean (across domain) pointwise variance (across curves). Values near 1 indicate registration removed original variability; values near 0 indicate little change; negative values indicate registration increased variability (sign something went wrong). Warp deviation identity (deciles across curves): curve's inverse warping function \(h_i^{-1}\) compared identity via normalized integral \( 2/L^2  \int |h_i^{-1}(t) - t|\, dt \), \(L\) domain length. normalizing constant \(L^2/2\) theoretical upper limit deviation monotone, domain-preserving warp maps timepoints first last timepoint, values range 0 (identity warp, time deformation) 1 (maximal crazy warping). Values \(\approx 0.3\) may suggest aggressive warping warrant inspection. Warp slopes (deciles per-curve min max \(dh^{-1}/dt\)): slope 1 warp corresponds local time deformation (identity). Slopes \(> 1\) indicate local time dilation (warped curve "stretched" relative template), slopes \(< 1\) indicate local time compression, slopes near 0 large slopes indicate extreme local deformation. affine shift warps, slopes exactly 1. Domain coverage loss (printed loss occurs): fraction original domain range lost per curve alignment, computed 1 - range(aligned_arg) / range(original_arg). relevant affine (non-domain-preserving) warps alignment can shift parts curves outside original domain. Domain-preserving methods (srvf, cc, landmark) always zero domain loss.

title: Registration Result Object — tf_registration
text: tf_aligned(x): extract registered/aligned curves (tfd vector). tf_inv_warps(x): extract estimated inverse warping functions \(h_i^{-1}(t)\) map observed time aligned time (tfd vector). Use tf_invert() result obtain forward warps needed. tf_template(x): extract template function (tf vector length 1).

title: Registration Result Object — tf_registration
text: Fabian Scheipl, Claude Opus 4.6

title: Gaussian Process random generator — tf_rgp
text: Generates n realizations zero-mean Gaussian process. function also accepts user-defined covariance functions (without "nugget" effect, see cov), implemented defaults scale parameter \(\phi\), order \(o\) nugget effect variance \(\sigma^2\) : squared exponential: \(Cov(x(t), x(t')) = \exp(-(t-t')^2)/\phi) + \sigma^2 \delta_{t}(t')\). Wiener process: \(Cov(x(t), x(t')) = \min(t',t)/\phi + \sigma^2 \delta_{t}(t')\), Matèrn process: \(Cov(x(t), x(t')) = \tfrac{2^{1-o}}{\Gamma(o)} (\tfrac{\sqrt{2o}|t-t'|}{\phi})^o \text{Bessel}_o(\tfrac{\sqrt{2o}|t-t'|}{s}) + \sigma^2 \delta_{t}(t')\) Brownian Bridge process \(t, t' \[, b]\): \(Cov(x(t), x(t')) =  \frac{(b - \max(s,t))(\min(s, t) - )}{\phi (b - )} + \sigma^2 \delta_{t}(t')\)

title: Gaussian Process random generator — tf_rgp
code: tf_rgp(   n,   arg = 51L,   cov = c("squareexp", "wiener", "matern", "brown_bridge"),   scale = diff(domain)/10,   nugget = scale/200,   order = 1.5,   domain = NULL )

title: Gaussian Process random generator — tf_rgp
text: n many realizations draw. arg vector evaluation points (arg return object). Defaults (0, 0.02, 0.04, ..., 1). given single integer (forget L...), creates  regular grid length (0,1). given n-long list vectors, irregular functional data created. cov type covariance function use. Implemented defaults "squareexp", "wiener", "matern", see description. Can also vectorized function returning \(Cov(x(t), x(t'))\) without nugget effect pairs inputs t t'. scale scale parameter (see description). Defaults width domain divided 10. nugget nugget effect additional white noise / unstructured variability. Defaults scale/200 (: little white noise). order order Matèrn covariance (used, must >0), defaults 1.5. higher, smoother process. Evaluation covariance function becomes numerically unstable large (>20) order, use "squareexp". domain generated functions. provided, range supplied arg values.

1. define scoring function (simple weighted LS fit) fpca_scores <- function(data_matrix, efunctions, mean, weights) {   w_mat <- matrix(weights, ncol = length(weights), nrow = nrow(data_matrix),                   byrow = TRUE)   w_mat[is.na(data_matrix)] <- 0   data_matrix[is.na(data_matrix)] <- 0   data_wc <- t((t(data_matrix) - mean) * sqrt(t(w_mat)))   t(qr.coef(qr(efunctions), t(data_wc) / sqrt(weights))) } # 2. define wrapper for fpca_sc: fpca_sc_wrapper <- function(data, arg, pve = 0.995, ...) {   data_mat <- tfd(data) |> as.matrix(interpolate = TRUE)   fpca <- refund::fpca.sc(     Y = data_mat, argvals = attr(data_mat, "arg"), pve = pve, ...   )   c(fpca[c("mu", "efunctions", "scores", "npc")],     scoring_function = fpca_scores) } x_pc <- tfb_fpc(x_df, method = fpca_sc_wrapper) lines(x_pc, col = 2, lty = 2)  # }

Almost exact re-representation objects using Fourier- B-spline bases, fda-style bases implemented . tfb_spline(fdSmooth): convert fdSmooth objects. Almost exact re-representation objects using Fourier- B-spline bases, fda-style bases implemented . tfb_spline(tfd): convert tfd (raw functional data) tfb_spline(tfb): convert tfb: modify basis representation, smoothing. tfb_spline(default): convert tfb: default method, returning prototype data missing

code: x <- 1:3 * tfd(data = 0:10, arg = 0:10) plot(x)  # this operator's 2nd argument is quite overloaded -- you can: # 1. simply extract elements from the vector if no second arg is given: x[1] #> tfd[1]: [0,10] -> [0,10] based on 11 evaluations each #> interpolation by tf_approx_linear  #> [1]: ▁▁▂▃▄▄▅▆▇██ x[c(TRUE, FALSE, FALSE)] #> tfd[1]: [0,10] -> [0,10] based on 11 evaluations each #> interpolation by tf_approx_linear  #> [1]: ▁▁▂▃▄▄▅▆▇██ x[-(2:3)] #> tfd[1]: [0,10] -> [0,10] based on 11 evaluations each #> interpolation by tf_approx_linear  #> [1]: ▁▁▂▃▄▄▅▆▇██ # 2. use the second argument and optional additional arguments to #    extract specific function evaluations in a number of formats: x[1:2, c(4.5, 9)] # returns a matrix of function evaluations #>      4.5  9 #> [1,] 4.5  9 #> [2,] 9.0 18 #> attr(,"arg") #> [1] 4.5 9.0 x[1:2, c(4.5, 9), interpolate = FALSE] # NA for arg-values not in the original data #> Warning: ℹ `interpolate = FALSE` and no values present for some `j` #> ✖ `NA`s created. #>      4.5  9 #> [1,]  NA  9 #> [2,]  NA 18 #> attr(,"arg") #> [1] 4.5 9.0 x[-3, seq(1, 9, by = 2), matrix = FALSE] # same as x[1:2, tf_arg(x), matrix = FALSE] #> [[1]] #>    arg value #> 1    0     0 #> 2    1     1 #> 3    2     2 #> 4    3     3 #> 5    4     4 #> 6    5     5 #> 7    6     6 #> 8    7     7 #> 9    8     8 #> 10   9     9 #> 11  10    10 #>  #> [[2]] #>    arg value #> 1    0     0 #> 2    1     2 #> 3    2     4 #> 4    3     6 #> 5    4     8 #> 6    5    10 #> 7    6    12 #> 8    7    14 #> 9    8    16 #> 10   9    18 #> 11  10    20 #>  # title: Constructors for vectors of
text: Various constructor methods tfd-objects.tfd objects contain vectors function evaluations observed arg-values, either arg-values (tfd_reg) different arg-values (tfd_irreg). NA-functions represented NULL-entries list. tfd.matrix accepts numeric matrix one function per row (!). arg provided, tries guess arg column names falls back 1:ncol(data) fails. tfd.data.frame uses first 3 columns data id (function ID), arg (argument value) value (function value) default. tfd.list accepts list vectors identical lengths containing evaluations list 2-column matrices/data.frames arg first evaluations second column tfd.default returns class prototype argument tfd() NULL recognised class. .tfd_irreg converts regular tfd tfb objects irregular ones. Mainly used internally tf_rebase operations etc.

title: Constructors for vectors of
text: tfd-objects list-vctrs numeric vectors containing function evaluations. evaluator: must (quoted bare) name function signature function(x, arg, evaluations) returns functions' (approximated/interpolated) values locations x based function evaluations available locations arg. Available evaluator-functions: tf_approx_linear linear interpolation without extrapolation (.e., zoo::na.approx() na.rm = FALSE)  – default, tf_approx_spline cubic spline interpolation, (.e., zoo::na.spline() na.rm = FALSE), tf_approx_fill_extend linear interpolation constant extrapolation (.e., zoo::na.fill() fill = "extend") tf_approx_locf "last observation carried forward" (.e., zoo::na.locf() na.rm = FALSE) tf_approx_nocb "next observation carried backward" (.e., zoo::na.locf() na.rm = FALSE, fromLast = TRUE). See tf:::zoo_wrapper tf:::tf_approx_linear, simply zoo_wrapper(zoo::na.approx, na.rm = FALSE), examples implementations .

title: Constructors for vectors of
code: # turn irregular to regular tfd by evaluating on a common grid:  f <- c(   tf_rgp(1, arg = seq(0, 1, length.out = 11)),   tf_rgp(1, arg = seq(0, 1, length.out = 21)) ) #> Warning: Combining incompatible <tfd_reg> with <tfd_reg> by casting to <tfd_irreg>. tfd(f, arg = seq(0, 1, length.out = 21)) #> New names: #> • `1` -> `1...1` #> • `1` -> `1...2` #> tfd[2]: [0,1] -> [-2.005736,1.048938] based on 21 evaluations each #> interpolation by tf_approx_linear  #> 1...1: ▅▅▅▆▆▇▇█████▇▆▅▄▃▃▂▃▃ #> 1...2: ▄▃▃▂▁▁▁▁▁▂▃▄▅▆▆▆▆▇▇▇█  set.seed(1213) f <- tf_rgp(3, arg = seq(0, 1, length.out = 51)) |> tf_sparsify(0.9) # does not yield regular data because linear extrapolation yields NAs #   outside observed range: tfd(f, arg = seq(0, 1, length.out = 101)) #> Warning: ℹ 86 evaluations were `NA` #> ✖ Returning irregular <tfd>. #> irregular tfd[3]: [0,1] -> [-0.7846883,1.067128] based on 59 to 87 (mean: 72) evaluations each #> interpolation by tf_approx_linear  #> 1: (0.20, 0.50);(0.21, 0.48);(0.22, 0.46); ... #> 2: (0.12,-0.34);(0.13,-0.30);(0.14,-0.26); ... #> 3: (0.04,-0.43);(0.05,-0.43);(0.06,-0.42); ... # this "works" (but may not yield sensible values..!!) for #   e.g. constant extrapolation: tfd(f, evaluator = tf_approx_fill_extend, arg = seq(0, 1, length.out = 101)) #> tfd[3]: [0,1] -> [-0.7846883,1.067128] based on 101 evaluations each #> interpolation by tf_approx_fill_extend  #> 1: ▆▆▆▆▆▆▆▅▅▄▄▄▃▃▂▂▂▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁▁ #> 2: ▂▂▂▃▄▄▅▆▇███████████▇▇▆▅▄▃ #> 3: ▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▂▃▃▃▃▃▃▃▃▃▃▃ plot(f, col = 2) tfd(f,   arg = seq(0, 1, length.out = 151), evaluator = tf_approx_fill_extend ) |> lines()

title: tf 0.4.0
text: tf_register() & co implement registration methods (SRVF, affine, landmark, continuous-criterion) aligning functions analyzing phase variability. Returns new tf_registration class accessors tf_aligned(), tf_inv_warps(), tf_template(). tf_depth() now implements five functional depth methods: MBD (default), MHI, FM (Fraiman-Muniz), FSD (Functional Spatial Depth), RPD (Regularized Projection Depth). MHI ranks functions lowest (0) highest (1); depths scaled 0 (extreme) 1 (central). MBD now scaled [0, 1] instead [0, 0.5]. tf_split() / tf_combine() splitting functions sub-domain fragments re-combining tf_rgp() gains "brownian_bridge" covariance option can generate irregular data via new irreg argument tfb_spline() now converts fda::fd fda::fdSmooth objects directly mgcv-style Fourier basis constructor use tfb_spline() sparklines print() / format() tf subtypes tf_where() now defaults arg = tf_arg(x), consistent functions .matrix() irregular tfd now always interpolates common grid

title: tf 0.4.0
text: tf_derive() tfd objects now uses second-order accurate finite differences, preserving original domain grid (#59) major overhaul NA handling: NA entries now consistently represented NULL internally; fixes tf_arg(), tf_evaluate(), tf_smooth(), tfd() conversion, .na(), printing, arithmetic NA entries fix summary.tf() returning wrong central range fix tfd domain enforcement length-1 data (#139) fix printing empty tfb objects -NA vectors

title: tf 0.4.0
text: tf_derive tf_integrate can now handle tfb non-identity link functions falling back tfd-calculus methods (returning tfd objects). tfd_irreg arithmetic now operates intersection arg-values instead failing arg-values identical tfb arithmetic fix dropped glue dependency favour cli / sprintf; replaced stopifnot() informative cli::cli_abort() throughout use vctrs::vec_arith() group generics, vctrs utils [.tf various internal performance improvements use air formatter R code
